## 3. Data Model — Full DDL + RLS (First Supabase Migration)

This is the deduped, security-corrected schema. Copy-paste runnable as `supabase/migrations/0000_init.sql`. Incorporates: G1 (`*_minor` bigint), G3–G8, H3 (no anon-INSERT), H4 (`ig_oauth_states`), H9/M (token-hook contract), I/M1 (Vault), H, C, forgotten-#4 (audit), L7.

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- 00_extensions_and_schemas.sql
-- ============================================================
create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";   -- gen_random_uuid, gen_random_bytes, digest, hmac
create extension if not exists "citext";
-- Supabase Vault ("supabase_vault") preinstalled on platform; verify in local CLI (see D2 / risk).

create schema if not exists app;
revoke all on schema app from anon, authenticated;

-- ============================================================
-- 01_enums.sql
-- ============================================================
create type store_status            as enum ('pending','active','suspended','archived');
create type member_role             as enum ('owner','admin','staff');
create type global_role             as enum ('user','superadmin');
create type product_status          as enum ('draft','published','archived');
create type order_status            as enum ('pending','confirmed','fulfilled','cancelled','refunded');
create type fulfillment_status      as enum ('unfulfilled','partial','fulfilled');
create type payment_status          as enum ('unpaid','pending','paid','partially_refunded','refunded','failed');
create type payment_method          as enum ('cod','stripe');
create type payment_provider_status as enum ('requires_action','processing','succeeded','failed','refunded');
-- text+CHECK (cheap to extend):
--   products.source        in ('instagram','manual')
--   ig_accounts.provider   in ('instagram','facebook')

-- ============================================================
-- 02_profiles.sql   (1:1 with auth.users)
-- ============================================================
create table public.profiles (
  id           uuid primary key references auth.users(id) on delete cascade,
  email        citext,
  full_name    text,
  avatar_url   text,
  global_role  global_role not null default 'user',
  created_at   timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at   timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- L7: only copies name/avatar; global_role is NOT taken from user metadata.
create or replace function app.handle_new_user()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
begin
  insert into public.profiles (id, email, full_name, avatar_url)
  values (new.id, new.email,
          new.raw_user_meta_data ->> 'full_name',
          new.raw_user_meta_data ->> 'avatar_url')
  on conflict (id) do nothing;
  return new;
end; $$;
create trigger on_auth_user_created
  after insert on auth.users for each row execute function app.handle_new_user();

-- H9: prevent the row owner from changing their own global_role; only service-role/superadmin may.
create or replace function app.guard_profile_role()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
begin
  if new.global_role is distinct from old.global_role then
    if not (coalesce((auth.jwt() -> 'app_metadata' ->> 'global_role') = 'superadmin', false)
            or auth.role() = 'service_role') then
      raise exception 'global_role may only be changed by superadmin/service_role';
    end if;
  end if;
  return new;
end; $$;
create trigger trg_guard_profile_role
  before update on public.profiles for each row execute function app.guard_profile_role();

-- ============================================================
-- 03_stores_and_members.sql
-- ============================================================
-- L2: reserved-subdomain denylist enforced in DB (defense-in-depth) + app layer.
create table public.reserved_subdomains (name citext primary key);
insert into public.reserved_subdomains (name) values
  ('app'),('api'),('admin'),('www'),('assets'),('cdn'),('static'),
  ('mail'),('ftp'),('ns1'),('ns2'),('status'),('help'),('docs'),('blog'),('dashboard');

create table public.stores (
  id               uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  owner_id         uuid not null references public.profiles(id) on delete restrict,
  subdomain        citext not null unique
                     check (subdomain ~ '^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{1,61}[a-z0-9])?$'),
  name             text not null,
  status           store_status not null default 'pending',
  base_currency    char(3) not null default 'USD',
  default_country  char(2),
  active_theme_id  uuid,                                   -- FK added after themes (06)
  -- checkout/orders settings (resolves H):
  track_inventory     boolean not null default false,
  auto_confirm_on_paid boolean not null default false,
  next_order_seq      bigint  not null default 1,
  notify_email        text,
  payment_methods     payment_method[] not null default '{cod}',   -- D-H1
  created_at       timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at       timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- L2: reject reserved names at write time.
create or replace function app.guard_subdomain()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
begin
  if exists (select 1 from public.reserved_subdomains r where r.name = new.subdomain) then
    raise exception 'subdomain "%" is reserved', new.subdomain;
  end if;
  return new;
end; $$;
create trigger trg_guard_subdomain
  before insert or update of subdomain on public.stores
  for each row execute function app.guard_subdomain();

create table public.store_members (
  store_id   uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  user_id    uuid not null references public.profiles(id) on delete cascade,
  role       member_role not null default 'staff',
  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  primary key (store_id, user_id)
);
create index idx_store_members_user on public.store_members(user_id);

create or replace function app.sync_store_owner_membership()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
begin
  insert into public.store_members (store_id, user_id, role)
  values (new.id, new.owner_id, 'owner')
  on conflict (store_id, user_id) do update set role = 'owner';
  return new;
end; $$;
create trigger trg_store_owner_membership
  after insert on public.stores for each row execute function app.sync_store_owner_membership();

-- ============================================================
-- 04_ig_accounts.sql   (I/M1: Vault — store ONLY the secret UUID)
-- ============================================================
create table public.ig_accounts (
  id                     uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  store_id               uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  provider               text not null default 'instagram'
                           check (provider in ('instagram','facebook')),
  ig_user_id             text not null,
  ig_username            text,
  name                   text,                  -- display name (C/H)
  account_type           text,
  profile_picture_url    text,                  -- last-seen expiring CDN url
  profile_picture_storage_path text,            -- re-hosted in store-media (theming source)
  media_count            int,
  fb_page_id             text,                  -- FB-login only
  -- token lifecycle (M1: Vault — NO encrypted bytea, NO IG_TOKEN_ENC_KEY)
  access_token_secret_id uuid,                  -- -> vault.secrets.id
  token_type             text default 'bearer',
  scopes                 text[] not null default '{}',
  token_expires_at       timestamptz,
  token_refreshed_at     timestamptz,
  token_status           text not null default 'active'
                           check (token_status in ('active','expiring','expired','revoked','error')),
  last_sync_at           timestamptz,
  last_sync_cursor       text,
  last_sync_error        text,
  connected_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
  deauthorized_at        timestamptz,
  data_deletion_requested_at timestamptz,
  created_at             timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at             timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (store_id),        -- v1: one IG account per store (I); drop later for multi
  unique (ig_user_id)
);
create index idx_ig_accounts_refresh on public.ig_accounts (token_status, token_expires_at);

-- ============================================================
-- 04b_ig_oauth_states.sql   (H4: CSRF/state binding — was referenced, never defined)
-- ============================================================
create table public.ig_oauth_states (
  nonce       text primary key,                  -- random, also set in httpOnly cookie
  store_id    uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  user_id     uuid not null references public.profiles(id) on delete cascade,
  provider    text not null default 'instagram',
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  expires_at  timestamptz not null default now() + interval '10 minutes',
  used_at     timestamptz
);
create index idx_ig_oauth_states_expiry on public.ig_oauth_states (expires_at);

-- ============================================================
-- 05_catalog.sql
-- ============================================================
create table public.products (
  id              uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  store_id        uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  source          text not null default 'manual' check (source in ('instagram','manual')),
  ig_media_id     text,
  ig_permalink    text,
  ig_posted_at    timestamptz,                   -- H: added
  status          product_status not null default 'draft',
  title           text not null,
  slug            text not null,
  description     text,
  tags            text[] not null default '{}',
  price_minor     bigint not null default 0 check (price_minor >= 0),  -- G1
  currency        char(3) not null default 'USD',
  stock           integer check (stock is null or stock >= 0),         -- null = untracked
  image_url       text,                          -- convenience: primary published image (signed at render)
  position        integer not null default 0,
  created_at      timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at      timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (store_id, slug),
  unique (store_id, ig_media_id)
);
create index idx_products_store_status on public.products(store_id, status);
create index idx_products_store_pub    on public.products(store_id) where status = 'published';
create index idx_products_store_igtime on public.products(store_id, ig_posted_at desc) where source='instagram';

create table public.product_images (
  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  product_id    uuid not null references public.products(id) on delete cascade,
  store_id      uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,  -- denorm RLS
  storage_path  text not null,                   -- object path in 'store-media' bucket
  alt           text,
  position      integer not null default 0,
  is_video      boolean not null default false,  -- C/H: theming skips videos
  video_url     text,                            -- mp4 (re-hosted optional)
  created_at    timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index idx_product_images_product on public.product_images(product_id, position);
create index idx_product_images_store   on public.product_images(store_id);

create table public.product_variants (
  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  product_id    uuid not null references public.products(id) on delete cascade,
  store_id      uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  title         text not null,
  sku           text,
  price_minor   bigint check (price_minor is null or price_minor >= 0),  -- null = inherit
  stock         integer check (stock is null or stock >= 0),
  position      integer not null default 0,
  created_at    timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (store_id, sku)
);
create index idx_variants_product on public.product_variants(product_id);

-- ============================================================
-- 06_themes.sql   (A: versioned + active_theme_id pointer; B: tokens jsonb)
-- ============================================================
create table public.themes (
  id              uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  store_id        uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  version         integer not null,
  tokens          jsonb not null default '{}'::jsonb,   -- DesignTokens (theming owns shape)
  logo            jsonb not null default '{}'::jsonb,   -- ThemeLogo (processedPath etc.)
  meta            jsonb not null default '{}'::jsonb,   -- model, sourceMediaIds, fallbackUsed, contrastAdjusted
  source_post_ids text[] not null default '{}',
  model           text,
  generated_at    timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (store_id, version)
);
create index idx_themes_store on public.themes(store_id);

alter table public.stores
  add constraint stores_active_theme_fk
  foreign key (active_theme_id) references public.themes(id) on delete set null;

-- ============================================================
-- 07_customers.sql
-- ============================================================
create table public.customers (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  store_id    uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  user_id     uuid references public.profiles(id) on delete set null,  -- null = guest
  email       citext,
  name        text,
  phone       text,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (store_id, email)
);
create index idx_customers_store on public.customers(store_id);
create index idx_customers_user  on public.customers(user_id);

-- ============================================================
-- 08_orders.sql   (G1 *_minor bigint, G3/G5/G6/G7/G8)
-- ============================================================
create table public.orders (
  id                 uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  store_id           uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  customer_id        uuid references public.customers(id) on delete set null,
  order_number       text not null,                       -- INS-<seq> (G8)
  status             order_status not null default 'pending',
  fulfillment_status fulfillment_status not null default 'unfulfilled',
  payment_status     payment_status not null default 'unpaid',
  payment_method     payment_method not null default 'cod',
  currency           char(3) not null,
  -- money (G1: minor/bigint, checkout's complete set)
  subtotal_minor     bigint not null default 0 check (subtotal_minor >= 0),
  shipping_minor     bigint not null default 0 check (shipping_minor >= 0),
  discount_minor     bigint not null default 0 check (discount_minor >= 0),
  tax_minor          bigint not null default 0 check (tax_minor      >= 0),
  total_minor        bigint not null default 0 check (total_minor    >= 0),
  -- contact + shipping snapshot (G6: checkout's complete column set)
  contact_email      citext,
  contact_phone      text,
  contact_name       text,
  ship_name          text,
  ship_line1         text,
  ship_line2         text,
  ship_city          text,
  ship_region        text,
  ship_postcode      text,
  ship_country       char(2),
  customer_note      text,
  -- guest read-back: stored random token (D-G7 decision = stored access_token; M4)
  access_token       text not null default encode(gen_random_bytes(24),'hex'),
  -- idempotency + stripe seam (G5)
  idempotency_key          text,
  stripe_session_id        text,
  stripe_payment_intent_id text,
  -- restock idempotency (M3)
  restocked_at       timestamptz,
  placed_at          timestamptz not null default now(),
  confirmed_at       timestamptz,
  fulfilled_at       timestamptz,
  cancelled_at       timestamptz,
  created_at         timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at         timestamptz not null default now(),
  constraint orders_number_uq unique (store_id, order_number),
  constraint orders_idem_uq   unique (store_id, idempotency_key)
);
create index idx_orders_store_status  on public.orders(store_id, status, placed_at desc);
create index idx_orders_store_payment on public.orders(store_id, payment_status);
create index idx_orders_customer      on public.orders(customer_id);

create table public.order_items (
  id                 uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id           uuid not null references public.orders(id) on delete cascade,
  store_id           uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,  -- denorm RLS
  product_id         uuid references public.products(id) on delete set null,
  variant_id         uuid references public.product_variants(id) on delete set null,
  title_snapshot     text not null,
  image_url_snapshot text,
  unit_price_minor   bigint not null check (unit_price_minor >= 0),  -- server-trusted at checkout
  quantity           integer not null check (quantity > 0),
  line_total_minor   bigint not null check (line_total_minor >= 0),
  created_at         timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index idx_order_items_order on public.order_items(order_id);
create index idx_order_items_store on public.order_items(store_id);

-- ============================================================
-- 09_payments.sql   (G4/G5: enum method + stripe_* columns)
-- ============================================================
create table public.payments (
  id                       uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  store_id                 uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  order_id                 uuid not null references public.orders(id) on delete cascade,
  method                   payment_method not null,
  provider_status          payment_provider_status,           -- null for cod
  amount_minor             bigint not null check (amount_minor >= 0),
  currency                 char(3) not null,
  stripe_payment_intent_id text,
  stripe_charge_id         text,
  received_at              timestamptz,
  recorded_by              uuid references auth.users(id),     -- staff who marked cash paid
  raw                      jsonb,
  created_at               timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index idx_payments_order on public.payments(order_id);
create index idx_payments_store on public.payments(store_id);
create unique index payments_pi_uq on public.payments(stripe_payment_intent_id)
  where stripe_payment_intent_id is not null;

-- ============================================================
-- 10_order_events.sql + webhook_events.sql (audit, idempotency)
-- ============================================================
create table public.order_events (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id    uuid not null references public.orders(id) on delete cascade,
  store_id    uuid not null references public.stores(id) on delete cascade,
  actor_id    uuid references auth.users(id),   -- null = system/webhook/customer
  kind        text not null,                    -- 'created','status_change','payment','note'
  from_value  text,
  to_value    text,
  meta        jsonb,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index idx_order_events_order on public.order_events(order_id, created_at);

create table public.webhook_events (   -- L4: Stripe idempotency ledger
  id           text primary key,        -- stripe event.id (evt_...)
  type         text not null,
  store_id     uuid,
  processed_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- ============================================================
-- 11_audit_log.sql   (forgotten-#4: membership/role/IG/theme audit)
-- ============================================================
create table public.audit_log (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  store_id    uuid references public.stores(id) on delete set null,
  actor_id    uuid references auth.users(id),
  action      text not null,    -- 'ig.connect','ig.disconnect','ig.token_refresh_fail',
                                 -- 'member.add','member.remove','member.role_change',
                                 -- 'theme.generate','theme.revert'
  target      text,
  meta        jsonb,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index idx_audit_store on public.audit_log(store_id, created_at desc);

-- ============================================================
-- 12_updated_at.sql
-- ============================================================
create or replace function app.touch_updated_at()
returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
begin new.updated_at = now(); return new; end; $$;
create trigger t_profiles_updated   before update on public.profiles   for each row execute function app.touch_updated_at();
create trigger t_stores_updated     before update on public.stores     for each row execute function app.touch_updated_at();
create trigger t_ig_accounts_updated before update on public.ig_accounts for each row execute function app.touch_updated_at();
create trigger t_products_updated   before update on public.products   for each row execute function app.touch_updated_at();
create trigger t_orders_updated     before update on public.orders     for each row execute function app.touch_updated_at();
```

### RLS helpers (private `app` schema)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- 20_rls_helpers.sql
-- ============================================================
create or replace function app.is_superadmin()
returns boolean language sql stable security definer set search_path = '' as $$
  select coalesce((auth.jwt() -> 'app_metadata' ->> 'global_role') = 'superadmin', false);
$$;

create or replace function app.has_store_access(p_store_id uuid, p_min_role member_role default 'staff')
returns boolean language sql stable security definer set search_path = '' as $$
  select exists (
    select 1 from public.store_members m
    where m.store_id = p_store_id and m.user_id = (select auth.uid())
      and (case m.role when 'owner' then 3 when 'admin' then 2 else 1 end)
          >= (case p_min_role when 'owner' then 3 when 'admin' then 2 else 1 end)
  );
$$;

-- L: thin boolean alias for checkout/multitenancy callers; canonical policies use app.has_store_access
create or replace function public.is_store_member(p_store uuid)
returns boolean language sql stable security definer set search_path = '' as $$
  select app.has_store_access(p_store, 'staff');
$$;

revoke all on function app.is_superadmin()                     from anon, authenticated;
revoke all on function app.has_store_access(uuid, member_role) from anon, authenticated;
grant execute on function app.is_superadmin()                  to authenticated;
grant execute on function app.has_store_access(uuid, member_role) to authenticated;
grant execute on function public.is_store_member(uuid)         to authenticated;
```

### RLS policies (security-corrected)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- 30_rls_enable.sql
-- ============================================================
alter table public.profiles         enable row level security;
alter table public.stores           enable row level security;
alter table public.store_members    enable row level security;
alter table public.ig_accounts      enable row level security;
alter table public.ig_oauth_states  enable row level security;
alter table public.products         enable row level security;
alter table public.product_images   enable row level security;
alter table public.product_variants enable row level security;
alter table public.themes           enable row level security;
alter table public.customers        enable row level security;
alter table public.orders           enable row level security;
alter table public.order_items      enable row level security;
alter table public.payments         enable row level security;
alter table public.order_events     enable row level security;
alter table public.audit_log        enable row level security;
-- reserved_subdomains, webhook_events: no RLS; service-role only (no anon/auth grants).
revoke all on public.reserved_subdomains from anon, authenticated;
revoke all on public.webhook_events      from anon, authenticated;

-- 31_profiles
create policy "profiles: self read"   on public.profiles for select to authenticated
  using ( (select auth.uid()) = id or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "profiles: self update" on public.profiles for update to authenticated
  using ( (select auth.uid()) = id )
  with check ( (select auth.uid()) = id );  -- H9: global_role change blocked by guard trigger

-- 32_stores
create policy "stores: public active read" on public.stores for select to anon, authenticated
  using ( status = 'active' );
create policy "stores: member read"  on public.stores for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "stores: admin update" on public.stores for update to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(id,'admin') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(id,'admin') or app.is_superadmin() );
-- store creation = service-role only (signup flow validates subdomain). No INSERT policy. (H10)

-- 33_store_members  (H10: writes owner-only; no self-insert path for authenticated)
create policy "members: read same store" on public.store_members for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "members: owner manage"    on public.store_members for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'owner') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'owner') or app.is_superadmin() );

-- 34_ig_accounts  (token never a column → never exposed; admin-only read)
create policy "ig: admin read" on public.ig_accounts for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'admin') or app.is_superadmin() );
-- writes: service-role only (no authenticated write policy).

-- 34b_ig_oauth_states: service-role only (no policies). Verified in /api/ig/callback server-side. (H4)

-- 35_products
create policy "products: public read published" on public.products for select to anon, authenticated
  using ( status = 'published'
          and exists (select 1 from public.stores s where s.id = store_id and s.status='active') );
create policy "products: staff read all" on public.products for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "products: staff write"    on public.products for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );

-- 36_product_images / variants
create policy "images: public read published" on public.product_images for select to anon, authenticated
  using ( exists (select 1 from public.products p where p.id = product_id and p.status='published')
          and exists (select 1 from public.stores s where s.id = store_id and s.status='active') );
create policy "images: staff all" on public.product_images for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "variants: public read published" on public.product_variants for select to anon, authenticated
  using ( exists (select 1 from public.products p where p.id = product_id and p.status='published') );
create policy "variants: staff all" on public.product_variants for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );

-- 37_themes  (public reads ONLY the active theme of an active store)
create policy "themes: public read active" on public.themes for select to anon, authenticated
  using ( exists (select 1 from public.stores s where s.active_theme_id = themes.id and s.status='active') );
create policy "themes: staff read all" on public.themes for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "themes: admin write"    on public.themes for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'admin') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'admin') or app.is_superadmin() );

-- 38_customers  (H10/forgotten-#8: staff store-scoped; self-read own row only)
create policy "customers: staff all" on public.customers for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "customers: self read" on public.customers for select to authenticated
  using ( user_id = (select auth.uid()) );

-- 39_orders  (H3: NO anon INSERT. Guest writes go through place_order() RPC ONLY.)
create policy "orders: staff all" on public.orders for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "orders: customer self read" on public.orders for select to authenticated
  using ( customer_id in (select c.id from public.customers c where c.user_id = (select auth.uid())) );
-- anon: NO select, NO insert. Guest read-back via order_lookup() RPC; guest write via place_order().

-- 40_order_items  (H3: NO anon INSERT)
create policy "order_items: staff all" on public.order_items for all to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() )
  with check ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "order_items: customer self read" on public.order_items for select to authenticated
  using ( order_id in (
    select o.id from public.orders o join public.customers c on c.id=o.customer_id
    where c.user_id = (select auth.uid())) );

-- 41_payments  (service-role only writes; staff read)
create policy "payments: staff read" on public.payments for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );

-- 42_order_events / audit_log (staff read; writes via RPC/service-role)
create policy "order_events: staff read" on public.order_events for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
create policy "audit: staff read" on public.audit_log for select to authenticated
  using ( app.has_store_access(store_id,'staff') or app.is_superadmin() );
```

### Server-side RPCs (the secure write seams)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- 50_decrement_stock.sql   (M3: atomic; null stock = untracked)
-- ============================================================
create or replace function public.decrement_stock(p_product uuid, p_store uuid, p_qty int)
returns boolean language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
declare ok boolean;
begin
  update public.products set stock = stock - p_qty
   where id = p_product and store_id = p_store
     and (stock is null or stock >= p_qty)
  returning true into ok;
  return coalesce(ok, false);
end; $$;
revoke all on function public.decrement_stock(uuid,uuid,int) from anon, authenticated;

-- ============================================================
-- 51_place_order.sql   (H3/G7/M3/M9: THE ONLY guest write path. Reprices server-side.)
-- p_lines: jsonb array of {product_id, qty}. Prices are NEVER trusted from client.
-- ============================================================
create or replace function public.place_order(
  p_store      uuid,
  p_idem       text,
  p_contact    jsonb,      -- {email, phone, name}
  p_ship       jsonb,      -- {name,line1,line2,city,region,postcode,country}
  p_note       text,
  p_lines      jsonb       -- [{product_id, qty}]
) returns table (order_id uuid, order_number text, access_token text)
language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
declare
  v_store      public.stores;
  v_existing   public.orders;
  v_line       jsonb;
  v_prod       public.products;
  v_qty        int;
  v_subtotal   bigint := 0;
  v_seq        bigint;
  v_order_id   uuid;
  v_number     text;
  v_token      text;
  v_cust_id    uuid;
begin
  -- M9: validate store active
  select * into v_store from public.stores where id = p_store and status = 'active';
  if not found then raise exception 'STORE_INACTIVE'; end if;

  -- idempotency replay
  select * into v_existing from public.orders where store_id = p_store and idempotency_key = p_idem;
  if found then
    return query select v_existing.id, v_existing.order_number, v_existing.access_token; return;
  end if;

  -- find-or-create customer (forgotten-#8: scoped by store+email)
  if coalesce(p_contact->>'email','') <> '' then
    insert into public.customers (store_id, email, name, phone)
    values (p_store, p_contact->>'email', p_contact->>'name', p_contact->>'phone')
    on conflict (store_id, email) do update set name = excluded.name
    returning id into v_cust_id;
  end if;

  -- per-store order number (G8)
  update public.stores set next_order_seq = next_order_seq + 1
   where id = p_store returning next_order_seq - 1 into v_seq;
  v_number := 'INS-' || v_seq;
  v_token  := encode(gen_random_bytes(24),'hex');

  insert into public.orders (
    store_id, customer_id, order_number, currency, idempotency_key, access_token,
    contact_email, contact_phone, contact_name,
    ship_name, ship_line1, ship_line2, ship_city, ship_region, ship_postcode, ship_country,
    customer_note, payment_method, status, payment_status, subtotal_minor, total_minor
  ) values (
    p_store, v_cust_id, v_number, v_store.base_currency, p_idem, v_token,
    p_contact->>'email', p_contact->>'phone', p_contact->>'name',
    p_ship->>'name', p_ship->>'line1', p_ship->>'line2', p_ship->>'city',
    p_ship->>'region', p_ship->>'postcode', upper(left(coalesce(p_ship->>'country','  '),2)),
    p_note, 'cod', 'pending', 'unpaid', 0, 0
  ) returning id into v_order_id;

  -- reprice EVERY line from products; decrement stock atomically (H3/M3)
  for v_line in select * from jsonb_array_elements(p_lines) loop
    v_qty := (v_line->>'qty')::int;
    if v_qty < 1 then raise exception 'BAD_QTY'; end if;
    select * into v_prod from public.products
      where id = (v_line->>'product_id')::uuid and store_id = p_store and status = 'published';
    if not found then raise exception 'PRODUCT_UNAVAILABLE'; end if;

    if v_store.track_inventory then
      if not public.decrement_stock(v_prod.id, p_store, v_qty) then
        raise exception 'OUT_OF_STOCK';   -- rolls back whole txn
      end if;
    end if;

    insert into public.order_items (
      order_id, store_id, product_id, title_snapshot, image_url_snapshot,
      unit_price_minor, quantity, line_total_minor
    ) values (
      v_order_id, p_store, v_prod.id, v_prod.title, v_prod.image_url,
      v_prod.price_minor, v_qty, v_prod.price_minor * v_qty
    );
    v_subtotal := v_subtotal + v_prod.price_minor * v_qty;
  end loop;

  update public.orders set subtotal_minor = v_subtotal, total_minor = v_subtotal
    where id = v_order_id;  -- shipping/tax computed by caller in v1.1+; v1 = subtotal
  insert into public.order_events (order_id, store_id, actor_id, kind, to_value)
    values (v_order_id, p_store, null, 'created', 'pending');

  return query select v_order_id, v_number, v_token;
end; $$;
-- anon may EXECUTE the RPC but has NO table INSERT (H3).
grant execute on function public.place_order(uuid,text,jsonb,jsonb,text,jsonb) to anon, authenticated;

-- ============================================================
-- 52_order_lookup.sql   (M4: guest read-back by id + stored token, constant-time compare)
-- ============================================================
create or replace function public.order_lookup(p_order uuid, p_token text)
returns jsonb language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
declare v_order public.orders; v_items jsonb;
begin
  select * into v_order from public.orders where id = p_order;
  if not found then return null; end if;
  -- L1: constant-time-ish compare via digest equality (24-byte entropy already strong)
  if not (digest(v_order.access_token,'sha256') = digest(p_token,'sha256')) then
    return null;
  end if;
  select coalesce(jsonb_agg(to_jsonb(i.*)),'[]') into v_items
    from public.order_items i where i.order_id = v_order.id;
  return to_jsonb(v_order) || jsonb_build_object('items', v_items);
end; $$;
grant execute on function public.order_lookup(uuid,text) to anon, authenticated;

-- ============================================================
-- 53_admin_order_transition.sql + mark_order_paid_*  (state machine, M3)
-- ============================================================
create or replace function public.mark_order_paid_cod(p_order uuid, p_store uuid, p_actor uuid)
returns void language plpgsql security definer set search_path = '' as $$
declare v public.orders;
begin
  select * into v from public.orders where id=p_order and store_id=p_store for update;
  if not found then raise exception 'NOT_FOUND'; end if;
  if v.payment_status <> 'unpaid' then raise exception 'ILLEGAL_TRANSITION'; end if;
  update public.orders set payment_status='paid' where id=p_order;
  insert into public.payments (store_id, order_id, method, amount_minor, currency, received_at, recorded_by)
    values (p_store, p_order, 'cod', v.total_minor, v.currency, now(), p_actor);
  insert into public.order_events (order_id, store_id, actor_id, kind, from_value, to_value)
    values (p_order, p_store, p_actor, 'payment', 'unpaid', 'paid');
end; $$;
-- mark_order_paid_stripe (future) is the Stripe analog → same target state. See §7.
```

> **Vault token write/read** (M1) is done by Nitro using service-role, never in client-exposed SQL:
> `select vault.create_secret(:token,'ig_token_'||:store_id,'IG long-lived token');` → store the returned UUID in `ig_accounts.access_token_secret_id`; read via `select decrypted_secret from vault.decrypted_secrets where id = :secret_id;`.

---

## 4. Multi-Tenancy & Tenant Resolution

### Host parsing (H1: `xForwardedHost:false`, locked by construction)

```ts
// server/utils/tenant.ts  — ONE getTenantFromHost (H1: scaffold + multitenancy reconciled)
import type { H3Event } from 'h3'
import { getRequestHost } from 'h3'

export type Surface = 'marketing' | 'admin' | 'store'
export interface HostInfo { host: string; subdomain: string | null; kind: 'apex'|'reserved'|'store'|'foreign' }

export function parseHost(event: H3Event): HostInfo {
  const base = (useRuntimeConfig(event).public.appBaseDomain || 'lvh.me').split(':')[0]
  // H1: xForwardedHost MUST be false. Never trust client-supplied X-Forwarded-Host.
  const raw = (getRequestHost(event, { xForwardedHost: false }) ?? '').toLowerCase()
  const host = raw.split(':')[0]
  if (host === base || host === `www.${base}`) return { host, subdomain: null, kind: 'apex' }
  const suffix = `.${base}`
  if (!host.endsWith(suffix)) return { host, subdomain: null, kind: 'foreign' }
  const label = host.slice(0, -suffix.length)
  if (label.includes('.')) return { host, subdomain: label, kind: 'foreign' }       // L3: no nesting
  // RESERVED set imported from shared/tenancy/reserved.ts (single source, L2)
  return { host, subdomain: label, kind: RESERVED_SUBDOMAINS.has(label) ? 'reserved' : 'store' }
}
```

> **H1 test (required before any code ships):** a request to `attacker.lvh.me` carrying `X-Forwarded-Host: victim.insteshop.app` must resolve `event.context.store` = **attacker**, never victim. If a trusted proxy ever rewrites Host, validate `X-Forwarded-Host` against an allowlist of proxy IPs — never raw.

### Middleware (sets `store`/`surface`/`hostInfo`; resolves E)

```ts
// server/middleware/00.tenant.ts
export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
  const p = event.path
  if (p.startsWith('/_') || p.startsWith('/__')) return
  const info = parseHost(event)
  event.context.hostInfo = info
  if (info.kind === 'apex')     { event.context.surface = 'marketing'; event.context.store = null; return }
  if (info.kind === 'reserved') { event.context.surface = 'admin';     event.context.store = null; return } // app.* etc.
  if (info.kind === 'foreign' || !info.subdomain) { event.context.surface = 'marketing'; event.context.store = null; return }
  // kind === 'store'
  event.context.surface = 'store'
  const store = await lookupStoreBySubdomain(event, info.subdomain)   // L3: cache key is validated subdomain
  if (!store || store.status !== 'active')
    throw createError({ statusCode: 404, statusMessage: 'Store not found', data: { code: 'STORE_NOT_FOUND', subdomain: info.subdomain } })
  event.context.store = store
})
```

`lookupStoreBySubdomain` uses service-role pinned to the **unique** `subdomain` (the one legitimate service-role read of the global registry) and selects `id, subdomain, name, status, base_currency, active_theme_id, track_inventory, default_country, payment_methods`. Cached in Nitro storage (`useStorage('tenant')`, 60s TTL; 30s negative cache, key = validated subdomain — L3).

### The safe service-role-vs-RLS pattern (H2/D)

```ts
// server/utils/supabaseAdmin.ts  — the ONE service-role factory (resolves D)
import { serverSupabaseServiceRole } from '#supabase/server'
export function supabaseAdmin(event) { return serverSupabaseServiceRole(event) }

// server/utils/tenantDb.ts  — mandatory choke-point for service-role on tenant tables
export function tenantScoped(event) {
  const store = event.context.store
  if (!store?.id) throw createError({ statusCode: 400, statusMessage: 'No tenant in context' })
  const db = supabaseAdmin(event); const storeId = store.id
  return {
    from: (t: string) => db.from(t).select('*').eq('store_id', storeId),
    insert: (t: string, rows) => db.from(t).insert(
      Array.isArray(rows) ? rows.map(r => ({ ...r, store_id: storeId })) : { ...rows, store_id: storeId }),
    rpc: db.rpc.bind(db), storeId,
  }
}
```

| Surface | Client | Isolation enforced by |
|---|---|---|
| Admin read/write (`app.*`) | `serverSupabaseClient` (seller JWT) | RLS via `store_members` |
| **Storefront public reads** | **`serverSupabaseClient` anon + RLS** (H2) | RLS: only published/active rows; tokens/drafts never selectable by anon |
| Guest checkout write | `place_order()` RPC via `supabaseAdmin` | RPC reprices + stamps `store_id` from `event.context.store` (H3/M9) |
| IG/theme/webhooks/superadmin | `supabaseAdmin` via `tenantScoped` | forced `store_id` filter; signature-verified |

**Cardinal rule (H2):** every public storefront read goes through **anon + RLS**, not service-role. Service-role is reserved for writes with no JWT and platform jobs; on tenant tables it is *only* reachable via `tenantScoped()`. CI lint forbids raw `supabaseAdmin()` on tenant tables outside `server/utils/*` and forbids `/api/storefront/*` reading `store_id` from `readBody` (M9).

### Local dev + prod

- **Local:** `NUXT_PUBLIC_APP_BASE_DOMAIN=lvh.me:3000`, `nuxi dev --host` (binds 0.0.0.0). `lvh.me` + `*.lvh.me` → 127.0.0.1, no `/etc/hosts`. Vite `server.allowedHosts: ['.lvh.me']`.
- **Prod (D-DEPLOY = Vercel Pro, Node runtime):** apex + wildcard `*.insteshop.app` at Vercel; DNS at Vercel nameservers (required for wildcard TLS). `/api/**` run as **Node functions** (Anthropic SDK + service-role need Node, not edge). Explicit records for `app.`, `www.`, `api.` precede the wildcard. Hobby is non-commercial — must be Pro.

---

## 5. Instagram Integration

**Verified 2026 reality:** Basic Display API is dead (2024-12-04). Two live options; we **primary on Instagram Login** (`graph.instagram.com`, no FB Page required) with FB Login as optional fallback — **D-J1 escalated to product owner** (diverges from the literal "Facebook Login" wording, schema supports both via `ig_accounts.provider`). Graph version pinned `v23.0` for any `graph.facebook.com` calls; `graph.instagram.com` business-login endpoints are unversioned.

### OAuth flow (Instagram Login, primary) — with H4 state binding

```
Seller(app.*)         Nitro /api/ig/connect → /api/ig/callback                 Meta/IG
  click Connect ─────▶ GET /api/ig/connect
                         authZ: app.has_store_access(store_id,'admin')          (H4)
                         nonce = random; INSERT ig_oauth_states(nonce,store_id,user_id,expires)
                         set httpOnly cookie ig_state=nonce
                       302 → www.instagram.com/oauth/authorize?...&scope=instagram_business_basic&state=nonce
  grant ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────▶
  302 ?code&state ───▶ GET /api/ig/callback
                         verify: state==cookie==DB nonce, unexpired, unused;     (H4)
                         verify auth.uid()==states.user_id AND has_store_access(store_id,'admin');
                         mark nonce used (single-use)
                         POST api.instagram.com/oauth/access_token (code→short, server-side)
                         GET graph.instagram.com/access_token?grant_type=ig_exchange_token (→long 60d)
                         GET graph.instagram.com/me?fields=id,username,name,account_type,profile_picture_url,media_count
                         vault.create_secret(token) → ig_accounts.access_token_secret_id  (M1)
                         audit_log('ig.connect'); enqueue first sync + profile-pic rehost
                       302 → /admin/connected
```

> **H4 (critical):** `store_id` is never trusted from `state` alone. The callback re-checks that the *currently authenticated* user minted the nonce **and** has admin access to that store. Without `ig_oauth_states` the CSRF mitigation was vapor.

### Scopes (verified)

| Scope (Instagram Login) | Grants | v1? | App Review |
|---|---|---|---|
| `instagram_business_basic` | profile + read `/me/media` (caption, media_url, children, permalink, timestamp) | **YES — only this** | Advanced Access → review required |
| content_publish / manage_comments / messages / insights | post/comment/DM/insights | No (over-requesting = #1 rejection cause) | skip |

FB-Login fallback (only if D-J1 flips): `instagram_basic`, `pages_show_list`, `pages_read_engagement` on `graph.facebook.com/v23.0`.

### Token lifecycle + at-rest storage (M1/M2)

- **At rest: Supabase Vault** (D2 resolved → Vault). `ig_accounts` stores only `access_token_secret_id uuid`. No `access_token_encrypted`, no `IG_TOKEN_ENC_KEY`.
- **Refresh:** daily Nitro task (D-CRON = Vercel Cron → secret-protected `GET /api/ig/_cron/refresh`) refreshes long-lived tokens at **T-10 days** (`token_expires_at < now()+interval '10 days'`). 400 on refresh ⇒ `token_status='error'` + `audit_log('ig.token_refresh_fail')`. Decrypt via `vault.decrypted_secrets`; rotate by updating the vault secret.
- **M2 (token-in-URL):** Meta requires `access_token` as a query param on these endpoints. A log-redaction middleware **must** scrub `access_token`, `client_secret`, `code`, `signed_request`, and `sk-ant-`/`sb_secret_` prefixes from all HTTP/error logs (Sentry URL scrubbing on). Refresh job logs status only, never the token.

### Media import mapping + image re-hosting (SSRF-hardened, H7)

`GET graph.instagram.com/me/media?fields=id,caption,media_type,media_url,thumbnail_url,permalink,timestamp,children{id,media_type,media_url,thumbnail_url}&limit=50`. Carousels expand to multiple `product_images` (one per child, `position` = order); video → `thumbnail_url` as image + `media_url` in `video_url`, `is_video=true`.

| Product field | Source | Rule |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | caption | first line / first ~80 chars; else `"Imported post {short-id}"` |
| `description` / `tags` | caption | full caption; `#(\w+)` → tags (escaped, no eval) |
| `price_minor` | caption regex `(\d+[.,]?\d*)\s?(EGP\|LE\|\$\|USD)` | if found set; else **null → admin must set before publish** (R5) |
| `status` | — | always `'draft'` |
| `source`/`ig_media_id`/`ig_permalink`/`ig_posted_at` | — | unique `(store_id, ig_media_id)` → idempotent upsert |

**Re-host every image** (CDN URLs expire — R3/C): `fetch(media_url)` server-side → upload to `store-media/{store_id}/products/{ig_media_id}/{i}.jpg`; store object path in `product_images.storage_path`. Profile pic → `ig_accounts.profile_picture_storage_path`. **Theming reads these re-hosted copies, never the expiring CDN** (resolves C).

**H7 SSRF + image-bomb hardening (applies to IG re-host AND theming download):**
- https only; **allowlist** hosts (`*.cdninstagram.com`, `*.fbcdn.net`, Supabase Storage). Resolve DNS, **pin the IP, connect to the pinned IP** (defeats DNS-rebinding); reject private/loopback/link-local (`169.254/16`, `10/8`, `127/8`, `::1`).
- Enforce `Content-Type: image/*`, max `Content-Length` 15 MB, `sharp(buf,{limitInputPixels:268402689})` + timeout.

### Webhooks, deauth, data-deletion (H5: signature-verified)

| Route | Auth | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /api/ig/webhook` | `hub.verify_token` | challenge echo |
| `POST /api/ig/webhook` | **`X-Hub-Signature-256` HMAC-SHA256 over `readRawBody`** (H5) | reject on mismatch; enqueue incremental sync |
| `POST /api/ig/deauthorize` | **verify `signed_request` with app secret** (H5) | resolve target by `ig_user_id` in *verified* payload; `token_status='revoked'`, `deauthorized_at`; keep products; `audit_log` |
| `POST /api/ig/data-deletion` | **verify `signed_request`** (H5/L5) | **enqueue** soft-delete + async job (not synchronous destructive); revoke Vault secret + delete `store-media/{store_id}/...` IG objects; return `{url, confirmation_code}`; `audit_log` |

All three: rate-limited per store (L6) + logged. Raw-body requirement mirrors the Stripe webhook.

### Dev-before-approval strategy

Add ourselves + seed sellers as **app testers** → Standard Access works with **no App Review**, against live endpoints. Build/test `connect/callback/sync/disconnect` + refresh + deauth/deletion fully. Local OAuth redirect must be HTTPS public → use `cloudflared`/`ngrok` tunnel registered as `NUXT_IG_REDIRECT_URI`. Submit for Advanced Access on `instagram_business_basic` only once the flow + privacy policy + data-deletion endpoint demonstrably work (the screencast shows exactly that). Business Verification of Insteshop's Meta business starts early (2–4 wk review latency, R1).

### `/api/ig/*` routes

```
server/api/ig/
  connect.get.ts          callback.get.ts        sync.post.ts (==old import; resolves J)
  disconnect.post.ts      webhook.get.ts         webhook.post.ts
  deauthorize.post.ts     data-deletion.post.ts  _cron/refresh.get.ts (secret-header gated)
```
Per-store rate limit on `/sync` (L6) prevents noisy-neighbor IG throttling (~200/hr/user).

---

## 6. Theming Pipeline

Turns IG post images + profile pic into a validated per-tenant `DesignTokens`, applied to a shared Tailwind v4 scaffold at runtime (no rebuild, no FOUC).

### Token TypeScript interface (`shared/types/theme.ts` — theming owns; B/D)

```ts
export const THEME_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1 as const
export const ALLOWED_HEADING_FONTS = ['Inter','Poppins','Montserrat','Playfair Display','Fraunces','Lora','Space Grotesk','DM Serif Display','Archivo','Libre Franklin'] as const
export const ALLOWED_BODY_FONTS    = ['Inter','Source Sans 3','Work Sans','Nunito Sans','DM Sans','Lora','Karla','IBM Plex Sans','Public Sans','Mulish'] as const
export const ALLOWED_RADIUS  = ['none','sm','md','lg','xl','full'] as const
export const ALLOWED_DENSITY = ['compact','cozy','comfortable'] as const
export const ALLOWED_BUTTON  = ['solid','soft','outline','pill'] as const
export const ALLOWED_MOOD    = ['minimal','luxury','playful','bold','earthy','vibrant','vintage','modern','elegant','rustic','techy','warm','cool','monochrome','pastel'] as const
type Hex = string  // validated /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/

export interface DesignTokens {
  palette: {
    primary:Hex; secondary:Hex; accent:Hex; bg:Hex; fg:Hex; muted:Hex; card:Hex; border:Hex
    neutral: Record<'50'|'100'|'200'|'300'|'400'|'500'|'600'|'700'|'800'|'900'|'950', Hex>
    onPrimary:Hex; onSecondary:Hex; onAccent:Hex
  }
  typography: { heading: typeof ALLOWED_HEADING_FONTS[number]; body: typeof ALLOWED_BODY_FONTS[number]
                feel:'serif'|'sans-display'|'geometric'|'humanist'|'monospace-accent'; scale:'tight'|'normal'|'airy' }
  radius: typeof ALLOWED_RADIUS[number]; density: typeof ALLOWED_DENSITY[number]
  buttonStyle: typeof ALLOWED_BUTTON[number]; shadow:'none'|'subtle'|'pronounced'
  mood: Array<typeof ALLOWED_MOOD[number]>; keywords: string[]    // keywords: admin TEXT display only (H6)
}
export interface ThemeLogo { source:'profile_picture'|'manual'|'post_extracted'; processedPath:string|null; faviconPath:string|null; originalPath:string|null; bgIsTransparent:boolean }
```

JSON example (`themes.tokens`):
```json
{"palette":{"primary":"#B5563A","secondary":"#2F3A36","accent":"#E0A458","bg":"#FAF6F0","fg":"#22201D","muted":"#6B6258","card":"#FFFFFF","border":"#E7DFD4","neutral":{"50":"#FAFAF9","100":"#F2F0EC","200":"#E5E1DA","300":"#D2CCC0","400":"#A89F90","500":"#807868","600":"#615A4E","700":"#494339","800":"#332F28","900":"#211E1A","950":"#15130F"},"onPrimary":"#FFFFFF","onSecondary":"#FFFFFF","onAccent":"#22201D"},"typography":{"heading":"Fraunces","body":"Karla","feel":"serif","scale":"airy"},"radius":"lg","density":"comfortable","buttonStyle":"soft","shadow":"subtle","mood":["earthy","warm","elegant"],"keywords":["handmade","ceramic","sunlit"]}
```

### Inputs (resolves C)

N=8 most-recent **re-hosted** post images from `product_images.storage_path` (joined via `products` where `source='instagram'`, ordered by `products.ig_posted_at desc`, skip `is_video=true`) + profile pic from `ig_accounts.profile_picture_storage_path`. Download + `sharp`-downscale to ≤1024px JPEG q80 (cuts vision tokens ~3×), apply H7 limits, then base64 to Claude (CDN copies never re-hit; resolves theming's expiry risk).

### Claude call (verified)

```ts
const res = await anthropic.messages.create({
  model: 'claude-opus-4-8',                       // $5/$25; sonnet-4-6 cost-down toggle
  max_tokens: 4000,
  system: [{ type:'text', text: SYSTEM_PROMPT, cache_control:{ type:'ephemeral' } }],  // byte-stable, cached
  output_config: { format: { type:'json_schema', schema: DESIGN_TOKENS_JSON_SCHEMA } }, // NOT output_format
  messages: [{ role:'user', content:[
    { type:'text', text: `Allowed heading fonts: ${ALLOWED_HEADING_FONTS.join(', ')}\nAllowed body fonts: ${ALLOWED_BODY_FONTS.join(', ')}\nAllowed mood: ${ALLOWED_MOOD.join(', ')}\nImage 1 is the PROFILE PICTURE (logo candidate). Images 2+ are recent posts. Return the design-token JSON.` },
    { type:'image', source:{ type:'base64', media_type:'image/jpeg', data: profileB64 } },
    ...postB64s.map(d => ({ type:'image' as const, source:{ type:'base64' as const, media_type:'image/jpeg' as const, data:d } })),
  ]}],
})
// M7: branch on stop_reason, null-guard stop_details
if (res.stop_reason === 'refusal') {
  logger.info('theme refusal', { category: res.stop_details?.category ?? null })  // null-guarded
  return FALLBACK_THEME
}
```

**System prompt** (frozen, cached): instructs base decisions only on visible imagery; real hex colors; fg/muted ≥ AA 4.5:1 on bg; onPrimary/onSecondary/onAccent readable; neutral scale tinted to brand temperature; fonts ONLY from allowed lists; mood 2–4 from allowed set; output ONLY the JSON object; if sparse/contradictory, return a tasteful neutral rather than refusing. Does **not** interpolate per-tenant data (cache stays byte-stable; M6). JSON schema can't enforce `minLength`/`pattern`/numeric — server-side Zod does (confirmed).

### Validation / clamp / contrast / fallback (H6 — single choke-point + fixed broken line)

```ts
// server/utils/theme/validate.ts — ALL writes go through persistTheme()→validateAndRepair (H6)
export function validateAndRepair(rawText: string): { tokens: DesignTokens; adjusted: boolean } {
  let parsed: unknown
  try { parsed = JSON.parse(rawText) } catch { throw new ThemeParseError() }
  const r = TokensZ.safeParse(parsed)              // Zod: hex regex + enum fonts/mood/radius/etc.
  if (!r.success) throw new ThemeParseError()       // caller → FALLBACK_THEME
  const t = r.data as DesignTokens
  let adjusted = false
  const bump = () => { adjusted = true }
  // H6: the broken contrast line is REWRITTEN to a clean call:
  t.palette.fg    = fixOn(t.palette.fg,    t.palette.bg, 4.5, bump)
  t.palette.muted = fixOn(t.palette.muted, t.palette.bg, 4.5, bump)
  t.palette.onPrimary   = bestOn(t.palette.primary)   // '#FFFFFF' | '#111111' by contrast
  t.palette.onSecondary = bestOn(t.palette.secondary)
  t.palette.onAccent    = bestOn(t.palette.accent)
  return { tokens: t, adjusted }
}
```

**Single choke-point (H6):** `persistTheme(tokens)` *always* runs `validateAndRepair`; `generate`, `PATCH /tokens` (manual override), `revert`, and any schema migration call it. **No raw write to `themes.tokens` anywhere.** `FALLBACK_THEME` (constant in code, `meta.fallbackUsed=true`) covers refusal/parse/Zod/API error/zero-images. Unit tests assert: (a) out-of-enum font → fallback; (b) a CSS-breakout string in a font field never appears in `tokensToCssVars` output; (c) contrast repair actually fires. `keywords`/`mood` are rendered as **text only** in admin (Vue `{{ }}`, never `v-html`) — stored-XSS via an IG caption is blocked (H6).

### CSS-variable application (SSR-safe, no FOUC; B)

```css
/* app/assets/css/main.css — Tailwind v4 @theme maps --color-* to runtime --t-* (B) */
@import "tailwindcss";
@theme {
  --color-primary: var(--t-primary, #111827);  --color-bg: var(--t-bg, #fff);  --color-fg: var(--t-fg, #18181b);
  --color-accent: var(--t-accent, #2563EB);    --color-on-primary: var(--t-on-primary, #fff);
  /* …secondary, muted, card, border, on-*, neutral-50..950… */
  --radius-brand: var(--t-radius, .5rem);
  --font-heading: var(--t-font-heading, ui-sans-serif), system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-body:    var(--t-font-body, ui-sans-serif), system-ui, sans-serif;
}
html { background: var(--color-bg); color: var(--color-fg); font-family: var(--font-body); }
[data-density="comfortable"]{ --space-card: 1.25rem } [data-btn="pill"] .btn{ border-radius:9999px }
[data-shadow="pronounced"] .card{ box-shadow:0 10px 30px -10px rgb(0 0 0/.25) }
```

```ts
// tokensToCssVars: ONLY interpolates validated values (H6 — never echoes keywords/mood free-text)
export function tokensToCssVars(t: DesignTokens): string {
  const radiusMap = { none:'0', sm:'.25rem', md:'.5rem', lg:'.875rem', xl:'1.25rem', full:'9999px' }
  const v: Record<string,string> = {
    '--t-primary':t.palette.primary, /* …all palette + on-* + neutral-*… */
    '--t-radius':radiusMap[t.radius],
    '--t-font-heading':`'${t.typography.heading}'`, '--t-font-body':`'${t.typography.body}'`,
  }
  return `:root{${Object.entries(v).map(([k,x])=>`${k}:${x}`).join(';')}}`
}
```

Injected **server-side** into `<head>` via a Nuxt server plugin reading `event.context.store.active_theme_id` → cached `themes` row (no client `setProperty` loop → no FOUC; B): `useHead({ htmlAttrs:{ 'data-density':t.density,'data-btn':t.buttonStyle,'data-shadow':t.shadow }, style:[{ innerHTML: tokensToCssVars(t), id:'tenant-theme' }], link: googleFontLinks(t.typography.heading, t.typography.body) })`. Fonts come from a static allowlist→Google `css2` map (only allowlisted fonts get a `<link>`; no injection). No theme row → emit `FALLBACK_THEME`. `data-*` attribute values are enum-validated (H6).

### Storage + regeneration

- Logo/source assets in **`store-assets`** bucket (private; signed URLs — M5): `{store_id}/logo/{original,processed,favicon-32,override}.png`, `{store_id}/theme/source/{media_id}.jpg`.
- `POST /api/theme/generate` writes a new immutable `themes` row (version+1) then repoints `stores.active_theme_id` (revert = pointer update). Per-store rate limit (L6: 10/day; ~$0.20–0.30/run on Opus, sonnet-4-6 halves it). Concurrency guard (advisory lock) prevents double-spend. `audit_log('theme.generate')`.

### `/api/theme/*` routes (all run server-side; M8)

```
server/api/theme/generate.post.ts   (admin; reads IG re-hosted images; Claude call is SERVER-ONLY)
server/api/theme/tokens.patch.ts     (admin; manual override → persistTheme → validate+contrast → version bump)
server/api/theme/logo.post.ts        (admin; upload override → logo.source='manual')
server/api/theme/revert.post.ts      (admin; {version} → repoint active_theme_id; audit 'theme.revert')
```
Live admin preview calls the server route — **never runs Claude client-side** (M8). `NUXT_CLAUDE_API_KEY`/`NUXT_SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY` referenced only under `server/` (CI lint, M8).

---

## 7. Checkout & Orders

Manual/COD first; data model is Stripe-ready (G5 columns already exist). Money = `*_minor bigint`.

### Cart (client-side, never authoritative)

Pinia store persisted to `localStorage`, keyed **`insteshop:cart:<subdomain>`** (resolves F — `subdomain`, not `slug`). `CartItem` carries display-only `unitPriceMinor` (server recomputes). Before showing summary, `POST /api/storefront/cart/price` revalidates lines (UX only — no trust placed in its result; `place_order` reprices again).

### Manual checkout sequence (H3/M9)

```
Customer(<store>.*)        Nitro /api/storefront/checkout                place_order() RPC
 fill contact+ship                                                       (SECURITY DEFINER)
 POST /cart/price ───▶ reprice from products (anon+RLS read)
 confirm
 POST /checkout {items:[{productId,quantity}], contact, ship, note,
                 idempotencyKey: crypto.randomUUID()} ──▶
   store = event.context.store              (server-resolved; NEVER from body — M9)
   db.rpc('place_order', { p_store: store.id, p_idem, p_contact, p_ship, p_note,
                           p_lines: items.map(i=>({product_id:i.productId, qty:i.quantity})) })
     → reprices every line from products, decrements stock atomically,    (H3/M3)
       inserts order+items+event in ONE txn, returns {order_id, order_number, access_token}
 201 { order, accessToken } ; clear cart
 (async, non-blocking) notifyNewOrder() via Resend
```

The handler passes only `event.context.store.id` (after H1) and body `{contact, ship, note, items, idempotencyKey}` — **no client `store_id`/prices**. `place_order` is the single guest write path (H3); anon has no table INSERT. Replay returns the existing order (idempotency `(store_id, idempotency_key)`). Guest read-back: `GET /api/storefront/orders/:id?token=` → `order_lookup(id, token)` RPC (M4, stored `access_token`, constant-time compare). Self-cancel (pending only): `POST /api/storefront/orders/:id/cancel` guarded by the token.

### Order/payment state machine + allowed transitions

Two orthogonal axes (COD can be `fulfilled` while `unpaid`; Stripe `paid` before `confirmed`):

```ts
export const ORDER_STATUS_TRANSITIONS = {
  pending:   ['confirmed','cancelled'],
  confirmed: ['fulfilled','cancelled'],
  fulfilled: ['refunded'],
  cancelled: [], refunded: [],
}
export const PAYMENT_STATUS_TRANSITIONS = {
  unpaid:['pending','paid'], pending:['paid','failed'],
  paid:['refunded','partially_refunded'], partially_refunded:['refunded'],
  refunded:[], failed:['unpaid'],
}
function assertTransition(map, from, to) {
  if (!map[from]?.includes(to)) throw createError({ statusCode:422, statusMessage:`ILLEGAL_TRANSITION ${from}->${to}` })
}
```

Cross-axis side effects (M3, enforced in RPC/service layer, one txn): `status→cancelled` restocks lines **idempotently** (guard on `orders.restocked_at` so a retried cancel can't double-restock). **Cancelling a paid order requires an explicit refund action — never implicitly flips `payment_status`** (M3 decision). `payment_status→paid` on `pending` leaves status as-is for COD; for Stripe, webhook may auto-advance `pending→confirmed` if `stores.auto_confirm_on_paid`. All transitions write `order_events`; membership/IG/theme changes write `audit_log`. Transitions are staff-only (`is_store_member` + RLS); webhooks act as system actor and may drive only payment transitions.

### Admin order management routes (seller JWT + RLS)

```
GET   /api/admin/orders            (?status=&payment_status=&q=&from=&to=&page=)
GET   /api/admin/orders/:id        (items + events + payments)
PATCH /api/admin/orders/:id/status (guarded transition map)
POST  /api/admin/orders/:id/mark-paid  (COD cash → mark_order_paid_cod RPC)
POST  /api/admin/orders/:id/refund     (payment_status→refunded; Stripe refund call later)
POST  /api/admin/orders/:id/notes      (order_events 'note')
GET   /api/admin/orders/stats          (counts by status, paid revenue, new-order badge)
```
New-order badge via Supabase Realtime on `orders` insert filtered by `store_id` (or 60s poll). Email via `server/utils/email.ts` (Resend; forgotten-#5/email-transport ownership).

### Stripe drop-in seam (additive only — no schema change)

Already present: `payment_method='stripe'`, `orders.stripe_session_id`/`stripe_payment_intent_id`, `payments.stripe_*` + `provider_status`, `webhook_events` ledger.

1. `checkout.post.ts` branches on `store.payment_methods`: after inserting the order in the **same `pending/unpaid`** state, `stripe.checkout.sessions.create({ mode:'payment', line_items: lines.map(l=>({quantity:l.qty, price_data:{currency:store.base_currency.toLowerCase(), unit_amount:l.unit_price_minor, product_data:{name:l.title}}})), success_url:`https://${store.subdomain}.insteshop.app/order/{CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`, client_reference_id:order.id, metadata:{order_id:order.id, store_id:store.id} }, { idempotencyKey: order.idempotency_key })` (resolves F — `subdomain`). Stock: decrement on `checkout.session.completed` (recommended) + abandoned-session sweep.
2. **New file** `server/api/webhooks/stripe.post.ts` (L4): `const raw = await readRawBody(event)` (route disables body parsing so signed bytes are exact), `stripe.webhooks.constructEventAsync(raw, sig, NUXT_STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)`, reject on bad signature; idempotency via `webhook_events` insert by `event.id`; on `checkout.session.completed` call `mark_order_paid_stripe(order_id, store_id, pi, amount, currency, raw)`. **Scope the lookup by `store_id` in metadata AND verify the order belongs to that store** (L4 — don't trust `client_reference_id` alone). Per-environment `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
3. `mark_order_paid_stripe` RPC = Stripe analog of COD `mark-paid` → identical target state (`payment_status='paid'`, conditional `confirmed`, insert `payments(method='stripe',...)`, `order_events('payment')`). COD and Stripe converge on identical order state, so admin UI/stats/refund are unchanged.

---

## 8. Nuxt Project Structure

**Versions (verify with `pnpm outdated` before lockfile):** Nuxt 4.4.x, `@nuxtjs/supabase` 2.x, Tailwind v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite`, `@pinia/nuxt` 0.11.x, `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (latest — verify model IDs `claude-opus-4-8`/`claude-sonnet-4-6`), `zod`, `sharp`, `stripe`, `resend`. One Nuxt app + Nuxt Layers (not a monorepo, not three deploys).

### Directory tree

```
insteshop/
├─ nuxt.config.ts  app.config.ts  package.json  .env.example  eslint.config.mjs  .nvmrc(22)
├─ app/
│  ├─ app.vue  error.vue
│  └─ assets/css/main.css                 # Tailwind v4 @theme → --t-* (§6)
├─ server/
│  ├─ middleware/00.tenant.ts             # sets store/surface/hostInfo (H1)
│  ├─ plugins/inject-theme.ts             # SSR <style> from active_theme_id (no FOUC)
│  ├─ plugins/log-redaction.ts            # scrub access_token/client_secret/code/keys (M2/M8)
│  ├─ utils/{tenant.ts,supabaseAdmin.ts,tenantDb.ts,claude.ts,email.ts,fetchImage.ts}
│  │                                       # fetchImage.ts = SSRF allowlist + IP-pin + sharp limits (H7)
│  ├─ utils/theme/{validate.ts,toCssVars.ts,fonts.ts,persist.ts}   # persist.ts = single choke-point (H6)
│  └─ api/
│     ├─ tenant/current.get.ts
│     ├─ ig/{connect,callback,sync,disconnect,deauthorize,data-deletion}.* webhook.{get,post}.ts _cron/refresh.get.ts
│     ├─ theme/{generate,tokens,logo,revert}.*
│     ├─ storefront/{cart/price.post.ts,checkout.post.ts,orders/[id].get.ts,orders/[id]/cancel.post.ts}
│     ├─ admin/orders/{index.get.ts,[id].get.ts,[id]/status.patch.ts,[id]/mark-paid.post.ts,[id]/refund.post.ts,[id]/notes.post.ts,stats.get.ts}
│     └─ webhooks/stripe.post.ts          # future
├─ shared/
│  ├─ tenancy/reserved.ts                 # RESERVED_SUBDOMAINS (single source — L2)
│  └─ types/{database.types.ts(generated),tenant.ts,theme.ts,orders.ts}
├─ layers/
│  ├─ base/      (Supabase wiring, UI primitives, composables useTenant/useTheme, plugins)
│  ├─ marketing/ (apex pages)
│  ├─ admin/     (dashboard, login, products, orders, settings/theme; middleware/auth.ts)
│  └─ storefront/(home, products/[slug], cart, checkout; stores/cart.ts)
├─ public/favicon.ico
└─ supabase/{config.toml, migrations/0000_init.sql, seed.sql}   # data-model owns migrations (resolves "unowned")
```

### Scaffold command sequence

```bash
brew install node && corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate
brew install supabase/tap/supabase      # Docker Desktop must be running for supabase start
pnpm dlx nuxi@latest init insteshop     # Nuxt 4 + app/
cd insteshop
pnpm add @nuxtjs/supabase @pinia/nuxt pinia @vueuse/core @anthropic-ai/sdk zod sharp stripe resend
pnpm add tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite
pnpm add -D @nuxt/eslint eslint typescript vue-tsc supabase
pnpm supabase init
pnpm dev
```

### `nuxt.config.ts` sketch

```ts
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  compatibilityDate: '2026-06-01',
  future: { compatibilityVersion: 4 },
  modules: ['@nuxtjs/supabase','@pinia/nuxt','@nuxt/eslint'],
  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
  vite: { plugins: [tailwindcss()], server: { allowedHosts: ['.lvh.me'] } },
  devServer: { host: '0.0.0.0', port: 3000 },
  supabase: {
    redirect: false,
    types: '~~/shared/types/database.types.ts',
    // H8: seller cookie HOST-SCOPED to app.* — NO Domain=.insteshop.app
    cookieName: 'sb',
    cookieOptions: { sameSite: 'lax', secure: true, maxAge: 60*60*8 },
  },
  runtimeConfig: {
    supabaseSecretKey: '', claudeApiKey: '', claudeModel: 'claude-opus-4-8', claudeFallbackModel: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
    igAppId: '', igAppSecret: '', igRedirectUri: '', igWebhookVerifyToken: '', igGraphVersion: 'v23.0',
    stripeSecretKey: '', stripeWebhookSecret: '', resendApiKey: '', resendFrom: '',
    public: { supabaseUrl: '', appBaseDomain: 'insteshop.app', siteUrl: 'https://insteshop.app' },
  },
  nitro: { preset: 'vercel', routeRules: { '/api/**': { cors: false } } },  // Node-runtime functions (D-DEPLOY)
  typescript: { typeCheck: false },  // vue-tsc in CI
})
```

### package.json dependency list (verify versions)

dependencies: `nuxt ^4.4`, `vue ^3.5`, `vue-router ^4.5`, `@nuxtjs/supabase ^2`, `@supabase/supabase-js ^2`, `@pinia/nuxt ^0.11`, `pinia ^3`, `@vueuse/core ^13`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (latest), `tailwindcss ^4.1`, `@tailwindcss/vite ^4.1`, `zod ^3.24`, `sharp`, `stripe`, `resend`.
devDependencies: `@nuxt/eslint ^1`, `eslint ^9`, `typescript ^5.7`, `vue-tsc ^2.2`, `supabase ^2`.
scripts: `dev:host`(nuxi dev --host), `db:start/stop/reset`, `db:types`(supabase gen types typescript --local > shared/types/database.types.ts), `typecheck`(vue-tsc --noEmit), `lint`.

## 9. Security Checklist (distilled from the security review)

**Critical/High — fix before any code ships:**
- [ ] **H1** Ship one `getTenantFromHost` with `xForwardedHost:false` hard-locked. Add a test: spoofed `X-Forwarded-Host` must not change the resolved tenant.
- [ ] **H2** All public storefront reads via **anon + RLS** (published/active only). Service-role reads only via `tenantScoped()`. CI lint bans raw `supabaseAdmin()` on tenant tables outside `server/utils/*`.
- [ ] **H3** Ship **no** anon-INSERT policies on `orders`/`order_items`. `place_order()` (SECURITY DEFINER, reprices every line) is the only guest write path; anon has EXECUTE on the RPC, no table INSERT.
- [ ] **H4** `ig_oauth_states` table exists; callback verifies nonce (cookie==DB, unexpired, single-use) AND `auth.uid()==minter` AND `has_store_access(store_id,'admin')`. Never trust `store_id` from `state` alone.
- [ ] **H5** Verify `X-Hub-Signature-256` (HMAC over `readRawBody`) on `/ig/webhook`; verify `signed_request` on `/ig/deauthorize` and `/ig/data-deletion`; data-deletion **enqueues** soft-delete + audit (recoverable). Rate-limit + log all three.
- [ ] **H6** One `persistTheme()` choke-point runs `validateAndRepair` on every path (generate/PATCH/revert/migration). Rewrite the broken contrast line. `tokensToCssVars` interpolates only validated (hex/enum) values. `keywords`/`mood` rendered as text, never `v-html`. Tests for out-of-enum font, CSS-breakout string, contrast fire.
- [ ] **H7** Server-side image fetch (IG re-host + theming): https-only host allowlist, DNS-resolve-then-pin-IP (anti-rebinding), reject private/loopback/link-local, `Content-Type: image/*`, max 15 MB, `sharp limitInputPixels` + timeout.
- [ ] **H8** Seller/admin auth cookie **host-scoped to `app.insteshop.app`** (no `Domain=.`). Customers get separate store-scoped sessions. Decided, not open.
- [ ] **H9** `app_metadata.global_role` set only via Custom Access Token Hook reading `profiles.global_role`; clients never set `app_metadata`. DB trigger blocks self-change of `profiles.global_role`. Force token refresh on demotion.
- [ ] **H10** `store_members` writes owner-only; explicit server-validated invite/accept flow; no self-insert path for `authenticated`. Test: customer JWT cannot insert into `store_members`, cannot read another store's orders/customers.

**Medium:**
- [ ] **M1** IG token at rest = Supabase Vault (`access_token_secret_id`); drop pgcrypto column + `IG_TOKEN_ENC_KEY`. Token never selectable by anon/auth (not a column).
- [ ] **M2** Log-redaction middleware scrubs `access_token`/`client_secret`/`code`/`signed_request`/API-key prefixes from all HTTP + error logs; Sentry URL scrubbing on.
- [ ] **M3** reprice+decrement in one txn (in `place_order`); cancel→restock idempotent via `restocked_at`; cancelling a paid order requires explicit refund.
- [ ] **M4** One guest-access mechanism: stored `orders.access_token` (24 bytes); `order_lookup` RPC selects by id AND token, constant-time compare; anon has no direct SELECT on orders. (HMAC scheme dropped — D-G7.)
- [ ] **M5** Storage: signed short-TTL URLs for anything not published; theme source + draft images private; Storage RLS mirrors table RLS; cross-tenant Storage test.
- [ ] **M6** Strict structured-output + clamp neutralizes caption/image prompt-injection; caption→product fields rendered as escaped text, price regex never `eval`, lengths capped.
- [ ] **M7** Branch on `stop_reason==='refusal'`; null-guard `stop_details`.
- [ ] **M8** Secrets only under `server/`; CI greps prod client bundle for `sk-ant-`/`sb_secret_`/IG secret; theming preview calls server route.
- [ ] **M9** `place_order` takes `event.context.store.id`, never body `store_id`; validates `store.status='active'`; grep no `/api/storefront/*` handler reads `store_id` from `readBody`.

**Hardening (L) + cross-cutting (forgotten):** L1 constant-time token compare; L2 reserved-subdomain denylist in DB trigger + shared module; L3 negative-cache keyed on validated subdomain; L4 Stripe raw-body + per-env secret + store-scoped lookup; L5 deletion also revokes Vault secret + IG storage objects; L6 per-store rate limits on `/theme/generate` + `/ig/sync`; L7 `handle_new_user` copies only name/avatar. **Strict CSP** (no inline scripts; theming `<style>` via nonce or scoped `style-src`), `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy`, HSTS. **Audit log** for IG connect/disconnect, token-refresh failures, membership/role changes, theme regenerations (order_events covers orders). Endpoint rate-limiting + replay protection on IG callbacks.
